family: Fries-, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement
rationale: This is the classic Fries rearrangement of phenyl acetate to 2-hydroxyacetophenone, a textbook example. It clearly demonstrates the migration of an acyl group from a phenolic oxygen to an ortho position on the aromatic ring, catalyzed by a Lewis acid (AlCl3). This example is simple, acyclic (except for the essential phenyl ring), and avoids complex functional groups, making it a clear and unambiguous representation of the core transformation.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses a simple phenyl group and an acetyl group, avoiding more complex or reactive motifs. It is distinct from Claisen rearrangements (which involve allyl ethers), benzidine rearrangements (which involve hydrazobenzenes), and general Friedel-Crafts acylations (which typically involve an arene and an acyl halide as separate reactants). The specific substrate-product relationship (phenolic ester to hydroxy aryl ketone) is characteristic of the Fries rearrangement.
